Title: The Expanse 1: Leviathan Wakes
Author: James S.A. Corey
Description: Epic. No other words.

Title: The Expanse 9: Leviathan Falls
Author: James S.A. Corey
Description: Space Opera as it should be written. This series unfolds in ways you never saw coming. Only problem with this series is that it came to an end.

Title: The Starless Sea
Author: Erin Morgenstern
Description: A magical underground library filled with forgotten legend and mystery ... need I say more?

Title: What Are You Willing to Risk?
Author: P L Camery

Title: For the Love of My Brother
Author: Sheilia Stodder

Title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
Author: J.K. Rowling
Description: Was feeling nostalgic and wanted a feel good book from my childhood.

Title: Project Hail Mary
Author: Andy Weir
Description: So good that it ruined Sci Fi for me. Writing this in June 2025 I'm still recovering.

Title: Lean In: Women, Work, and the Will to Lead
Author: Sheryl Sandberg

Title: Bobiverse 1: We Are Legion (We Are Bob)
Author: Dennis E. Taylor
Description: A standard 21st century software engineer nerd (Bob) dies young in a car accident. 100 years later he awakes to find that his conciousness has become the AI pilot of a space-faring drone whose mission it is to explore the cosmos for habitable planets and shepherd the human race off of a dying planet. Oh, and the fun part: he can clone himself.

Title: Armada
Author: Ernest Cline
Description: The man who brought us Ready Player One (an ALL TIME fave) brought us a wholesome gamer coming-of-age story but with alien invasions.

Title: Hunger Games 0: The Ballad of Songbirds and Snakes
Author: Suzanne Collins

Title: Dune
Author: Frank Herbert
Description: Only took me 27 years to read Dune, can I officially be a nerd now?

Title: Kingkiller Chronicle 1: The Name of the Wind
Author: Patrick Rothfuss

Title: Randomize
Author: Andy Weir
Description: Couldn't pass up a chance for a quick read from my fav.

Title: Hunger Games 0.5: Sunrise on the Reaping
Author: Suzanne Collins
Description: Depressing as heck. Good god.

Title: Bobiverse 2: For We Are Many
Author: Dennis E. Taylor
Description: The Others appear.

Title: Bobiverse 3: All These Worlds
Author: Dennis E. Taylor
Description: The Bobs face off with the Others.

Title: Bobiverse 4: Heaven's River
Author: Dennis E. Taylor
Description: There are soooooo many Bobs now and shit hits the fan. Civil war is brewing… And a crew of Bobs at the edge of the universe infiltrate an Alien civilization to rescue a Bob held hostage.

Title: The Prophet
Author: Kahlil Gibran
Description: Thoughtgful poems from 1923 about family, love, purpose, and life in general. Highly recommend!

Title: The Murder of Mr. Wickham (Mr. Darcy & Miss Tilney, #1)
Author: Claudia Gray
Description: Recommended for P&P fans. You know who you are.

Title: Pride and Prejudice
Author: Jane Austen
Description: Can we talk about how crazy it is that this writing is still so relevant over a hundred years later ??? There's a reason Jane Austen is still revered today, and it's this book. (That said, I will say the first 40% is really slow. Hold out for the good stuff!)

Title: The Lost Bookshop
Author: Evie  Woods
Description: Another magical book about books! And also about the perseverance of one really strong lady.

Title: Make Believe: Poems for Hoping Again
Author: Victoria Hutchins
Description: I was so so excited to read this, and I will say I did like 50% of the poems quite a bit. The other's just weren't for me.

Title: The Three-Body Problem (Remembrance of Earth‚Äôs Past, #1)
Author: Liu Cixin
Description: Three years ago I read Project Hail Mary which was so good it ruined all Sci-Fi for me. Three years later, this book brought me hope of a new dawn. I can't recommend it enough.

Title: The Dark Forest (Remembrance of Earth‚Äôs Past, #2)
Author: Liu Cixin
Description: Somehow better than the first ??

Title: Death's End (Remembrance of Earth‚Äôs Past, #3)
Author: Liu Cixin
Description: Somehow the best book in the series?? So rare that a series gets better and better as it progresses. This series is so multi-faceted I think I could read it repeatedly and get new perspectives each time.
